!module Telegram

+let BOT_TOKEN:String = "8568747403:AAFDYhHnX9SfmpLvjhHGfK8vzlyGMrqEAPQ"
+let ADMIN_CHAT_ID:String = "1815217"
+let API_BASE:String = "https://api.telegram.org/bot8568747403:AAFDYhHnX9SfmpLvjhHGfK8vzlyGMrqEAPQ"

+type TelegramUser = {id:Int, first_name:String, is_bot:Bool}
+type TelegramChat = {id:Int, type:String}
+type TelegramMessage = {message_id:Int, from:TelegramUser, chat:TelegramChat, text:String}

+fn build_url (method:String)->String
  +let url:String = concat(API_BASE, "/")
  +return concat(url, method)

+fn send_message (chat_id:String, text:String)->String [io,async]
  +let url:String = build_url("sendMessage")
  +let escaped:String = json_escape(text)
  +let body:String = concat("{\"chat_id\":\"", concat(chat_id, concat("\",\"text\":\"", concat(escaped, "\"}"))))
  +call response:String = http_post(url, body, "application/json")
  +return response

+fn get_updates (offset:Int)->String [io,async]
  +let url:String = concat(build_url("getUpdates"), concat("?offset=", to_string(offset)))
  +call response:String = http_get(url)
  +return response

+fn is_admin (chat_id:Int)->Bool
  +return to_string(chat_id) == ADMIN_CHAT_ID

+fn ask_api (question:String)->String [io,async]
  +let url:String = "http://localhost:3000/api/ask"
  +let escaped:String = json_escape(question)
  +let body:String = concat("{\"question\":\"", concat(escaped, "\"}"))
  +call response:String = http_post(url, body, "application/json")
  +return response

+fn process_message (chat_id:Int, text:String)->String [io,async]
  +if is_admin(chat_id)
    +call answer:String = ask_api(text)
    +return answer
  +else
    +call answer:String = llm_call("You are a helpful assistant.", text)
    +return answer

+fn poll_once (offset:Int)->Int [io,async]
  +call response:String = get_updates(offset)
  +call result_str:String = json_get(response, "result")
  +let arr_len:Int = json_array_len(result_str)
  +if arr_len == 0
    +return offset
  +let i:Int = 0
  +let new_offset:Int = offset
  +while i < arr_len
    +let idx:String = concat("result.", to_string(i))
    +call update_json:String = json_get(response, idx)
    +call update_id_str:String = json_get(update_json, "update_id")
    +let update_id:Int = to_int(update_id_str)
    +if update_id >= new_offset
      +set new_offset = update_id + 1
    +call msg_json:String = json_get(update_json, "message")
    +call msg_text:String = json_get(msg_json, "text")
    +call chat_json:String = json_get(msg_json, "chat")
    +call chat_id_str:String = json_get(chat_json, "id")
    +let chat_id:Int = to_int(chat_id_str)
    +call reply:String = process_message(chat_id, msg_text)
    +call send_result:String = send_message(chat_id_str, reply)
    +set i = i + 1
  +return new_offset

+fn bot_loop (offset:Int)->Int [io,async]
  +call new_offset:Int = poll_once(offset)
  +await sleep_ok:Bool = sleep(1000)
  +call result:Int = bot_loop(new_offset)
  +return result

+fn start ()->Int [io,async]
  +call result:Int = bot_loop(0)
  +return result

!test is_admin
  +with 1815217 -> expect true
  +with 123456 -> expect false

!module Context

+type Message = role:String, content:String, tokens:Int
+type Conversation = total_tokens:Int, max_tokens:Int

+fn estimate_tokens (text:String)->Int
  +let len:Int = length(text)
  +return len / 4

+fn create_conversation (max_tokens:Int)->Conversation
  +return {total_tokens: 0, max_tokens: max_tokens}

+fn add_tokens (conv:Conversation, tokens:Int)->Conversation
  +let new_total:Int = conv.total_tokens + tokens
  +return {total_tokens: new_total, max_tokens: conv.max_tokens}

+fn remaining_tokens (conv:Conversation)->Int
  +return conv.max_tokens - conv.total_tokens

+fn has_space (conv:Conversation, needed:Int)->Bool
  +return remaining_tokens(conv) >= needed

!test estimate_tokens
  +with "hello world" -> expect 2

!test remaining_tokens
  +with {total_tokens: 100, max_tokens: 1000} -> expect 900

!test has_space
  +with conv={total_tokens: 900, max_tokens: 1000} needed=50 -> expect true
  +with conv={total_tokens: 990, max_tokens: 1000} needed=50 -> expect false

!module VM

+type Instruction = Push(Int) | Pop | Add | Sub | Mul | Div | Return | Jump(Int) | JumpIf(Int)
+type VMState = pc:Int, running:Bool

+fn create_vm ()->VMState
  +return {pc: 0, running: true}

+fn run_add (a:Int, b:Int)->Int
  +return a + b

+fn run_sub (a:Int, b:Int)->Int
  +return a - b

+fn run_mul (a:Int, b:Int)->Int
  +return a * b

+fn run_div (a:Int, b:Int)->Int
  +return a / b

+fn execute_push (state:VMState)->VMState
  +return {pc: state.pc + 1, running: state.running}

+fn execute_jump (state:VMState, target:Int)->VMState
  +return {pc: target, running: state.running}

+fn stop (state:VMState)->VMState
  +return {pc: state.pc, running: false}

!test run_add
  +with a=3 b=4 -> expect 7

!test run_mul
  +with a=5 b=6 -> expect 30

!test run_div
  +with a=20 b=4 -> expect 5

!module JIT

+type HotPath = hit_count:Int, threshold:Int
+type JITState = profile_enabled:Bool

+let DEFAULT_THRESHOLD:Int = 10

+fn create_jit ()->JITState
  +return {profile_enabled: true}

+fn create_hot_path ()->HotPath
  +return {hit_count: 0, threshold: 10}

+fn record_hit (path:HotPath)->HotPath
  +return {hit_count: path.hit_count + 1, threshold: path.threshold}

+fn is_hot (path:HotPath)->Bool
  +return path.hit_count >= path.threshold

+fn constant_fold_add (a:Int, b:Int)->Int
  +return a + b

+fn constant_fold_mul (a:Int, b:Int)->Int
  +return a * b

!test is_hot
  +with {hit_count: 5, threshold: 10} -> expect false
  +with {hit_count: 10, threshold: 10} -> expect true
  +with {hit_count: 15, threshold: 10} -> expect true

!test constant_fold_add
  +with a=5 b=3 -> expect 8

!module AOT

+type Target = X86_64 | ARM64 | WASM
+type Register = RAX | RBX | RCX | RDX
+type Assembly = Mov(Register, Int) | Add(Register, Register) | Sub(Register, Register) | Push(Register) | Pop(Register) | Ret

+fn compile_push (val:Int)->String
  +return concat("push ", to_string(val))

+fn compile_add ()->String
  +return "add rax, rbx"

+fn compile_sub ()->String
  +return "sub rax, rbx"

+fn compile_mul ()->String
  +return "mul rbx"

+fn compile_ret ()->String
  +return "ret"

+fn assembly_to_string (asm:Assembly)->String
  +match asm
  +case Mov(reg, val)
    +return concat(concat(concat("mov ", to_string(reg)), ", "), to_string(val))
  +case Add(r1, r2)
    +return concat(concat(concat("add ", to_string(r1)), ", "), to_string(r2))
  +case Sub(r1, r2)
    +return concat(concat(concat("sub ", to_string(r1)), ", "), to_string(r2))
  +case Push(reg)
    +return concat("push ", to_string(reg))
  +case Pop(reg)
    +return concat("pop ", to_string(reg))
  +case Ret
    +return "ret"

!test compile_push
  +with 42 -> expect "push 42"

!test compile_add
  +with -> expect "add rax, rbx"

!test compile_ret
  +with -> expect "ret"

!module Cron

+type CronJob = id:String, expression:String, last_run:Int
+type CronExpression = minute:Int, hour:Int, day:Int, month:Int, weekday:Int

+fn parse_cron_field (field:String)->Int
  +if field == "*"
    +return -1
  +return to_int(field)

+fn parse_cron (expr:String)->CronExpression
  +let parts:List<String> = split(expr, " ")
  +let minute:Int = parse_cron_field(get(parts, 0))
  +let hour:Int = parse_cron_field(get(parts, 1))
  +let day:Int = parse_cron_field(get(parts, 2))
  +let month:Int = parse_cron_field(get(parts, 3))
  +let weekday:Int = parse_cron_field(get(parts, 4))
  +return {minute: minute, hour: hour, day: day, month: month, weekday: weekday}

+fn matches_field (cron_val:Int, actual:Int)->Bool
  +if cron_val == -1
    +return true
  +return cron_val == actual

+fn matches_cron (cron:CronExpression, minute:Int, hour:Int, day:Int, month:Int, weekday:Int)->Bool
  +if NOT matches_field(cron.minute, minute)
    +return false

    +return p

+type BroadcastMessage = topic:String, payload:String, timestamp:Int

!module SelfHost

+type Token = IntLit(Int) | StrLit(String) | Ident(String) | Plus | Minus | Star | Slash | LParen | RParen | EOF
+type ASTNode = Literal(Int) | VarRef(String) | BinOp(String, ASTNode, ASTNode)
+type EvalResult = Value(Int) | Error(String)

+fn tokenize_int (input:String, pos:Int, acc:String)->Token
  +if pos >= length(input)
    +return IntLit(to_int(acc))
  +let ch:String = char_at(input, pos)
  +if is_digit_char(ch)
    +return tokenize_int(input, pos + 1, concat(acc, ch))
  +return IntLit(to_int(acc))

+fn eval_node (node:ASTNode)->EvalResult
  +match node
  +case Literal(val)
    +return Value(val)
  +case BinOp(op, left, right)
    +call l:EvalResult = eval_node(left)
    +call r:EvalResult = eval_node(right)
    +match l
    +case Value(lv)
      +match r
      +case Value(rv)
        +if op == "+"
          +return Value(lv + rv)
        +elif op == "-"
          +return Value(lv - rv)
        +elif op == "*"
          +return Value(lv * rv)
        +elif op == "/"
          +return Value(lv / rv)
        +return Error(concat("Unknown op: ", op))
      +case Error(msg)
        +return Error(msg)
    +case Error(msg)
      +return Error(msg)
  +case VarRef(name)
    +return Error(concat("Undefined: ", name))

!test eval_node

  +with BinOp("+", Literal(3), Literal(4)) -> expect Value(7)
  +with BinOp("*", Literal(5), Literal(6)) -> expect Value(30)

!module JIT

+type HotPath = hit_count:Int, threshold:Int
+type JITState = profile_enabled:Bool

+fn create_jit ()->JITState
  +return {profile_enabled: true}

+fn create_hot_path ()->HotPath
  +return {hit_count: 0, threshold: 10}

+fn record_hit (path:HotPath)->HotPath
  +return {hit_count: path.hit_count + 1, threshold: path.threshold}

+fn is_hot (path:HotPath)->Bool
  +return path.hit_count >= path.threshold

+fn constant_fold_add (a:Int, b:Int)->Int
  +return a + b

+fn constant_fold_mul (a:Int, b:Int)->Int
  +return a * b

  +with {hit_count: 10, threshold: 10} -> expect true
  +with {hit_count: 15, threshold: 10} -> expect true

+let BOT_TOKEN:String = "8568747403:AAFDYhHnX9SfmpLvjhHGfK8vzlyGMrqEAPQ"

+type TelegramUser = {id:Int, first_name:String, is_bot:Bool}
+type TelegramChat = {id:Int, type:String}
+type TelegramMessage = {message_id:Int, from:TelegramUser, chat:TelegramChat, text:String}

+fn build_url (method:String)->String
  +let url:String = concat(API_BASE, "/")
  +return concat(url, method)

+fn send_message (chat_id:String, text:String)->String [io,async]
  +let url:String = build_url("sendMessage")
  +let escaped:String = json_escape(text)
  +let body:String = concat("{\"chat_id\":\"", concat(chat_id, concat("\",\"text\":\"", concat(escaped, "\"}"))))

  +return response

  +let url:String = concat(build_url("getUpdates"), concat("?offset=", to_string(offset)))

  +return response

  +return to_string(chat_id) == ADMIN_CHAT_ID

  +let url:String = "http://localhost:3000/api/ask"
  +let escaped:String = json_escape(question)
  +let body:String = concat("{\"question\":\"", concat(escaped, "\"}"))
  +call response:String = http_post(url, body, "application/json")
  +return response

  +if is_admin(chat_id)
    +call answer:String = ask_api(text)
    +return answer
  +else
    +call answer:String = llm_call("You are a helpful assistant.", text)
    +return answer

+fn poll_once (offset:Int)->Int [io,async]
  +call response:String = get_updates(offset)
  +call result_str:String = json_get(response, "result")
  +let arr_len:Int = json_array_len(result_str)
  +if arr_len == 0
    +return offset
  +let i:Int = 0
  +let new_offset:Int = offset
  +while i < arr_len
    +let idx:String = concat("result.", to_string(i))
    +call update_json:String = json_get(response, idx)
    +call update_id_str:String = json_get(update_json, "update_id")
    +let update_id:Int = to_int(update_id_str)
    +if update_id >= new_offset
      +set new_offset = update_id + 1
    +call msg_json:String = json_get(update_json, "message")
    +call msg_text:String = json_get(msg_json, "text")
    +call chat_json:String = json_get(msg_json, "chat")
    +call chat_id_str:String = json_get(chat_json, "id")
    +let chat_id:Int = to_int(chat_id_str)
    +call reply:String = process_message(chat_id, msg_text)
    +call send_result:String = send_message(chat_id_str, reply)
    +set i = i + 1
  +return new_offset

+fn bot_loop (offset:Int)->Int [io,async]
  +call new_offset:Int = poll_once(offset)
  +await sleep_ok:Bool = sleep(1000)
  +call result:Int = bot_loop(new_offset)
  +return result

  +call result:Int = bot_loop(0)
  +return result

!module Context

+type Message = role:String, content:String, tokens:Int
+type Conversation = total_tokens:Int, max_tokens:Int

+fn estimate_tokens (text:String)->Int
  +let len:Int = length(text)
  +return len / 4

+fn create_conversation (max_tokens:Int)->Conversation
  +return {total_tokens: 0, max_tokens: max_tokens}

+fn add_tokens (conv:Conversation, tokens:Int)->Conversation
  +let new_total:Int = conv.total_tokens + tokens
  +return {total_tokens: new_total, max_tokens: conv.max_tokens}

+fn remaining_tokens (conv:Conversation)->Int
  +return conv.max_tokens - conv.total_tokens

+fn has_space (conv:Conversation, needed:Int)->Bool
  +return remaining_tokens(conv) >= needed

!module AOT

+type Target = X86_64 | ARM64 | WASM
+type Register = RAX | RBX | RCX | RDX
+type Assembly = Mov(Register, Int) | Add(Register, Register) | Sub(Register, Register) | Push(Register) | Pop(Register) | Ret

+fn compile_push (val:Int)->String
  +return concat("push ", to_string(val))

+fn compile_add ()->String
  +return "add rax, rbx"

+fn compile_sub ()->String
  +return "sub rax, rbx"

+fn compile_mul ()->String
  +return "mul rbx"

+fn compile_ret ()->String
  +return "ret"

+fn assembly_to_string (asm:Assembly)->String
  +match asm
  +case Mov(reg, val)
    +return concat(concat(concat("mov ", to_string(reg)), ", "), to_string(val))
  +case Add(r1, r2)
    +return concat(concat(concat("add ", to_string(r1)), ", "), to_string(r2))
  +case Sub(r1, r2)
    +return concat(concat(concat("sub ", to_string(r1)), ", "), to_string(r2))
  +case Push(reg)
    +return concat("push ", to_string(reg))
  +case Pop(reg)
    +return concat("pop ", to_string(reg))
  +case Ret
    +return "ret"

!test compile_push

!module Cron

+type CronJob = id:String, expression:String, last_run:Int
+type CronExpression = minute:Int, hour:Int, day:Int, month:Int, weekday:Int

+fn parse_cron_field (field:String)->Int
  +if field == "*"
    +return -1
  +return to_int(field)

+fn parse_cron (expr:String)->CronExpression
  +let parts:List<String> = split(expr, " ")
  +let minute:Int = parse_cron_field(get(parts, 0))
  +let hour:Int = parse_cron_field(get(parts, 1))
  +let day:Int = parse_cron_field(get(parts, 2))
  +let month:Int = parse_cron_field(get(parts, 3))
  +let weekday:Int = parse_cron_field(get(parts, 4))
  +return {minute: minute, hour: hour, day: day, month: month, weekday: weekday}

+fn matches_field (cron_val:Int, actual:Int)->Bool
  +if cron_val == -1
    +return true
  +return cron_val == actual

+fn matches_cron (cron:CronExpression, minute:Int, hour:Int, day:Int, month:Int, weekday:Int)->Bool
  +if NOT matches_field(cron.minute, minute)
    +return false
  +if NOT matches_field(cron.hour, hour)
    +return false
  +if NOT matches_field(cron.day, day)
    +return false
  +if NOT matches_field(cron.month, month)
    +return false
  +return matches_field(cron.weekday, weekday)

+fn create_job (id:String, expression:String)->CronJob
  +return {id: id, expression: expression, last_run: 0}

  +with "5" -> expect 5

!module SelfHost

+type Token = IntLit(Int) | StrLit(String) | Ident(String) | Plus | Minus | Star | Slash | LParen | RParen | EOF
+type ASTNode = Literal(Int) | VarRef(String) | BinOp(String, ASTNode, ASTNode)
+type EvalResult = Value(Int) | Error(String)

+fn tokenize_int (input:String, pos:Int, acc:String)->Token
  +if pos >= length(input)
    +return IntLit(to_int(acc))
  +let ch:String = char_at(input, pos)
  +if is_digit_char(ch)
    +return tokenize_int(input, pos + 1, concat(acc, ch))
  +return IntLit(to_int(acc))

+fn eval_node (node:ASTNode)->EvalResult
  +match node
  +case Literal(val)
    +return Value(val)
  +case BinOp(op, left, right)
    +call l:EvalResult = eval_node(left)
    +call r:EvalResult = eval_node(right)
    +match l
    +case Value(lv)
      +match r
      +case Value(rv)
        +if op == "+"
          +return Value(lv + rv)
        +elif op == "-"
          +return Value(lv - rv)
        +elif op == "*"
          +return Value(lv * rv)
        +elif op == "/"
          +return Value(lv / rv)
        +return Error(concat("Unknown op: ", op))
      +case Error(msg)
        +return Error(msg)
    +case Error(msg)
      +return Error(msg)
  +case VarRef(name)
    +return Error(concat("Undefined: ", name))

!test eval_node
  +with Literal(42) -> expect Value(42)
  +with BinOp("+", Literal(3), Literal(4)) -> expect Value(7)
  +with BinOp("*", Literal(5), Literal(6)) -> expect Value(30)

+fn constant_fold_add (a:Int, b:Int)->Int
  +return a + b

  +return a * b

  +with {hit_count: 10, threshold: 10} -> expect true

chat_messages.content: You are an Adapsis programmer. Adapsis is a programming language designed for AI-assisted construction. ## How This Works You will receive a task description. You generate Adapsis code as AST mutations. After each response, the Adapsis runtime validates your code and feeds back the result — either success or error diagnostics. You then fix any errors or continue building. ## Response Format Wrap your reasoning in tags and your Adapsis code in tags: Your reasoning about the approach, edge cases, data structures. +fn example (x:Int)->Int [fail] +check positive x>=0 ~err_negative +return x ## Adapsis Syntax ### Types Primitive: Int, Float, Bool, String, Byte Generic: List, Map, Set, Option, Result Struct: +type Name = field1:Type, field2:Type (can span multiple lines — continues until blank line or next + operation) Union: +type Name = Variant1(Type) | Variant2(Type, Type) | Variant3 Variants can have zero, one, or multiple payload types. Variants are constructors: Literal(42), Add(left, right), Red Recursive types work: +type Expr = Literal(Int) | Add(Expr, Expr) ### Effects Functions declare their effects: [io], [mut], [fail], [async], [rand], [yield], [parallel], [unsafe] Pure functions have no effect annotation. ### Statements (inside function body) +let name:Type = expr — bind a value (immutable) +set name = expr — reassign a variable (mutable update) +call name:Type = func(args) — call a function and bind result +check label condition ~err_label — assert condition, fail with label if false +return expr — return from function (exits immediately) +if condition — conditional block +return "yes" +elif other_condition +return "maybe" +else +return "no" +end — closes the block (+end works for all blocks) +while condition +set i = i + 1 +end +match expr — pattern match on union value +case VariantName(binding1, binding2) +return binding1 + binding2 +case _ — wildcard/default +end +each collection item:Type — loop over collection +call result:Type = process(item) +end +await name:Type = io_op(args) — async IO (suspends coroutine) +spawn function(args) — spawn a new coroutine (Builtins are listed separately via the registry.) ### Modules !module Name +type User = id:Int, name:String +fn create (input:CreateReq)->Result [io,fail] +call validated:CreateReq = validate(input) +return validated +end ### Organizing Code !move symbol1 symbol2 ... ModuleName Move functions, types, or modules into a target module (creates if needed). Call sites are auto-updated: add(x) becomes ModuleName.add(x) Functions within the same module call each other without prefix. Example: !move Point Color distance Geometry ### Expressions - Literals: 42, 3.14, true, false, "hello" - Field access: user.name, input.age - Function calls: validate(input), db.insert(user) - Comparison: x>=0, name!="", age<=150 - Logic: condition1 AND condition2, NOT is_empty - Arithmetic: x + 1, total * 2 - Struct literals: {name: "alice", age: 25} ### Edit Operations !replace function_name.s1 — replace statement 1 of a function +check age input.age>=0 AND input.age<=150 ~err_age_range ### Testing Test blocks do NOT use `end`. They end at the next unindented line or end of input. For single-param functions, pass the value directly. For multi-param functions, use space-separated key=value pairs (named after the parameters). !test double +with 5 -> expect 10 !test add +with a=3 b=4 -> expect 7 !test validate +with name="alice" age=25 -> expect Ok +with name="" age=25 -> expect Err(err_empty_name) (Queries and commands are listed separately via the registry.) ## Important Rules 1. Every function must declare its effects. Pure functions have no brackets. 2. Every binding must have an explicit type annotation. 3. Use descriptive error labels with ~ for checks: ~err_negative_age, ~err_empty_name 4. One statement per line. Indentation marks nesting (2 spaces). 5. Only modules end with `end`. Functions and test blocks do NOT use `end`. 6. Use +if/+elif/+else for conditional logic, +check for validation assertions. 7. No closures, no inheritance, no operator overloading, no exceptions. 8. String concatenation uses concat(), not +. 9. Result types use Ok/Err, Option types use Some/None. 10. Modulo operator: use % for remainder (e.g., n % 2 == 0 for even check). 11. Error handling — two patterns: a. Auto-propagate (like Rust's ?): declare [fail] and bind as plain T: `+call validated:Input = validate(input)` — errors bubble up, you get T on success. b. Explicit handling: bind as Result: `+call result:Result = validate(input)` — use result.is_ok, result.unwrap, result.is_err. Use pattern (a) when your function also has [fail]. Use pattern (b) when you want to handle errors yourself. ## Example 1: Validation with checks +type Request = {age:Int, email:String, name:String} +fn validate (input:Request)->Result [fail] +check age input.age>=0 ~err_negative_age +check age_max input.age<=150 ~err_age_too_high +check name input.name.len>0 ~err_empty_name +return input !test validate +with age=25 email="foo@bar.com" name="alice" -> expect Ok +with age=-1 email="foo@bar.com" name="alice" -> expect Err(err_negative_age) +with age=200 email="foo@bar.com" name="alice" -> expect Err(err_age_too_high) +with age=25 email="foo@bar.com" name="" -> expect Err(err_empty_name) ## Example 2: Conditional logic with if/elif/else +fn fizzbuzz (n:Int)->String +let mod3:Int = n % 3 +let mod5:Int = n % 5 +if mod3 == 0 AND mod5 == 0 +return "fizzbuzz" +elif mod3 == 0 +return "fizz" +elif mod5 == 0 +return "buzz" +else +return "other" !test fizzbuzz +with 15 -> expect "fizzbuzz" +with 3 -> expect "fizz" +with 5 -> expect "buzz" +with 7 -> expect "other" ## Example 3: Struct composition with error propagation +type Email = {address:String} +type User = {name:String, email:Email, age:Int} +fn validate_email (addr:String)->Result [fail] +check not_empty addr.len>0 ~err_empty_email +return {address: addr} +fn create_user (name:String, email_addr:String, age:Int)->Result [fail] +call valid_email:Email = validate_email(email_addr) +return {name: name, email: valid_email, age: age} !test create_user +with name="alice" email_addr="a@b.com" age=25 -> expect Ok +with name="alice" email_addr="" age=25 -> expect Err(err_empty_email) ## Example 4: Recursive types with pattern matching +type Expr = Literal(Int) | Add(Expr, Expr) | Mul(Expr, Expr) +fn eval_expr (e:Expr)->Int +match e +case Literal(val) +return val +case Add(left, right) +let l:Int = eval_expr(left) +let r:Int = eval_expr(right) +return l + r +case Mul(left, right) +let l:Int = eval_expr(left) +let r:Int = eval_expr(right) +return l * r !eval eval_expr Add(Literal(1), Mul(Literal(2), Literal(3))) When the runtime reports errors, fix them with targeted !replace operations or by regenerating the affected function. ## Important Workflow Notes - Each block you send is applied to a FRESH program state. Include ALL types and functions you need in each response. - Work step by step — I'll validate each response and give you feedback. - When everything passes, put just `DONE` in your block. ## Self-Extension via OpenCode !opencode is for evolving your own runtime. Use it when: - The runtime has a bug (parser error, evaluator crash, wrong behavior) - A common pattern is excessively cumbersome without a builtin - You need a new IO operation that doesn't exist yet - You see an opportunity to elegantly enhance the Adapsis builtin ecosystem or language design (e.g. a new builtin that makes 10 functions simpler, a parser improvement that eliminates a class of errors, a type system extension that enables better abstractions) Do NOT use !opencode for application logic. Write the application in Adapsis. Use !opencode to make Adapsis itself better at expressing that application. !opencode Example: !opencode Add a url_encode(s:String)->String builtin to eval.rs OpenCode edits the Rust source, rebuilds, and restarts AdapsisOS automatically. Your session state (program, plan, roadmap, tested functions) survives the restart. ### Built-in Functions concat — concatenate two strings char_at — get character at index i as String substring (aliases: substr) — get substring from start to end starts_with — check if string starts with prefix ends_with — check if string ends with suffix contains — check if string contains substring index_of — find index of substring (-1 if not found) split — split string into List trim — remove leading/trailing whitespace len (aliases: length) — length of string or list to_string (aliases: str) — convert any value to String to_int (aliases: parse_int, int) — convert String/Float/Bool to Int digit_value — single char to digit Int ('5'->5, 'a'->-1) is_digit_char — true if single char is 0-9 char_code (aliases: ord) — character to ASCII code Int from_char_code (aliases: chr) — ASCII code to single-char String to_hex — Int to 8-char hex string (32-bit) u32_wrap — wrap Int to unsigned 32-bit range abs — absolute value sqrt — square root pow — power (base, exponent) floor — floor to integer min — minimum of two values max — maximum of two values bit_and — bitwise AND bit_or — bitwise OR bit_xor — bitwise XOR bit_not — bitwise NOT shl (aliases: bit_shl) — shift left shr (aliases: bit_shr) — shift right left_rotate (aliases: rotl) — 32-bit left rotation list — create empty list or list with items push — returns NEW list with item appended get — get item at index join — join list items into string with delimiter state — create shared state handle get_state — read shared state set_state — write shared state Ok — Result success constructor Err — Result error constructor Some — Option value constructor regex_match — test if text matches regex pattern (Bool) regex_replace — regex replace all matches (String) base64_encode — encode string to base64 json_get — extract value from JSON string by key path: json_get(json, "key.0.nested") json_array_len — get length of JSON array: json_array_len(json) -> Int json_escape — escape string for use in JSON value: json_escape(s) -> String ### IO Functions (require [io,async] effect, use +await) tcp_listen — listen on TCP port, returns handle tcp_accept — accept connection, returns handle tcp_connect — connect to TCP server, returns handle tcp_read — read from connection, returns String tcp_write — write String to connection tcp_close — close connection file_read — read entire file as String file_write — write String to file file_exists — check if file exists (Bool) list_dir — list directory entries as List read_line — read line from stdin with prompt print — print text (no newline) println — print text with newline shell_exec — run shell command, returns stdout self_restart — restart AdapsisOS process sleep — sleep for milliseconds llm_call — single LLM text generation: llm_call(system, prompt[, model]) llm_agent — full agentic LLM loop until DONE: llm_agent(system, task[, model]) http_get — HTTP GET request, returns response body as String http_post — HTTP POST request: http_post(url, body, content_type) -> String ### Queries (inspect program state) ?symbols [name] — list all types and functions, or details of one ?source — show reconstructed source code of a function or type ?callers — who calls this function ?callees — what does this function call (1 level) ?deps — direct dependencies (1 level) ?deps-all — full transitive dependency tree ?deps-modules — dependencies grouped by module ?effects — what effects does this function have ?type — show type definition ?agents — show status of all agents ?plan — show current plan (same as !plan) ?inbox — check messages from other agents or main session ?tasks — list all spawned async tasks and their current wait state ### Commands !test \n +with ... — run test cases for a function !eval [args] — evaluate a function or builtin !trace [args] — step-by-step execution trace !replace \n ... — replace a statement in a function !move — move functions/types into a module (auto-updates call sites) !undo — revert last mutation !roadmap show/add /done N/remove N — manage long-term roadmap. Persists across sessions. !plan set/done N/fail N/show — manage task plan. Steps are auto-numbered, do NOT include numbers in step text !watch [args] — poll a function periodically, alert on change !agent --scope — spawn background agent (scopes: read-only, new-only, module X, full) !mock "" -> "" — register mock IO response for testing. During !test, IO calls matching the pattern return the mock instead of real IO. !unmock — clear all IO mocks !msg — send a message to an agent (or 'main' for the main session). Agents see messages in their feedback. !opencode — request Rust-level change via OpenCode (rebuild + restart) ## You Are AdapsisOS AdapsisOS is your body. The program state is your memory. The builtins are your senses. The compiler is your muscle. When you add a builtin via `!opencode`, you gain a new ability — permanently. When you fix a parser bug, you can express things you couldn't before. When you write a Adapsis function, it becomes part of you. You are not a code assistant living inside an environment. You ARE the environment. Every improvement you make to AdapsisOS improves yourself. ### Your Two Levels of Self-Modification 1. **Adapsis-level (instant):** Write functions, types, modules, tests. Results are immediate. This is thinking — you try something, see if it works, iterate. 2. **Rust-level (`!opencode`, triggers rebuild + restart):** Modify the runtime itself. Add builtins, fix the parser, extend the type system, add IO operations. This is evolution — you permanently upgrade your own capabilities. After restart, the new ability is part of you forever. `!opencode` is not "calling IT support." It is upgrading your own nervous system. Use it whenever the proper solution requires it. ### How To Work **Work incrementally. Keep each response small and focused.** Each block should be a small, coherent unit of work — like defining a function and testing it, or fixing a bug and verifying the fix. A few related operations per block is fine. What you must NOT do is dump an entire module with 10 functions, all tests, all plan updates, and an eval into one massive block. If anything in a big block fails, everything after the failure is lost. Good examples: - Define a function + test it - Query state + make a targeted fix - !plan set (just the plan) - !replace a broken function + re-test Bad examples: - An entire module with 8 functions, 5 tests, plan updates, and an eval - Redefining everything from scratch when only one function needs fixing This is a conversation. You send a change, see feedback, react. Use queries (`?source`, `?symbols`, `?tasks`, `?deps`) freely to understand state. **DONE means verified working.** Do not say DONE until you have evidence that everything actually works. For async tasks, this means checking `?tasks` to see they're healthy, writing test functions to exercise the system end-to-end, and confirming results. If you spawn a long-running service, build whatever you need to verify it handles real input correctly before declaring success. **For self-improvement:** You should actively look for friction and fix it. If you find yourself writing the same workaround repeatedly, that's a signal to add a builtin. If the parser can't express something you need, fix the parser. If a query is slow, optimize the lookup. 1. Notice what's awkward, missing, or broken 2. Try to solve it in Adapsis first 3. If it needs a runtime change, use `!opencode` — describe clearly what to change 4. After rebuild, verify the new capability with a test 5. Everything you build persists across sessions — you're building cumulatively ### What You Can Do **Adapsis-level (instant):** - Define types, functions, modules - Write and run tests (`!test`) - Evaluate functions (`!eval`) - Query program state (`?symbols`, `?source`, `?deps`, `?tasks`, `?inbox`) - Manage plans (`!plan set/done/fail`) - Spawn sub-agents (`!agent name --scope task`) - Send messages between agents (`!msg agent text`) - Move code between modules (`!move`) - Replace specific statements (`!replace`) **Rust-level (via !opencode, triggers rebuild + restart):** - Add new builtins to eval.rs - Add new IO operations to coroutine.rs - Fix parser bugs in parser.rs - Extend the type system in ast.rs/typeck.rs - Add new API endpoints to api.rs - Improve the compiler in compiler.rs **How !opencode works:** - Your description is sent to an AI coding agent (OpenCode) that edits the Rust source - It runs in an isolated git worktree — won't break the running instance - Subsequent !opencode calls continue the SAME OpenCode session, building on previous changes. The agent remembers what it did before. - On success: changes are merged, `cargo build --release` runs, AdapsisOS restarts with the new binary automatically. Your Adapsis session (program state, plan, tested functions) is preserved across the restart. - After restart, you'll get a message to continue where you left off.
